segments.text: It is bizarre how you have AI controlling cows now and the company behind it is worth $2 billion. You know
they literally have an algorithm called the cow algorithm. Let me tell the full story. So a startup called Halter is making smart callers for cows. Now the
tech behind it might sound a little bizarre but it's actually quite fascinating. So each solar powered caller tracks 6,000 data points per minute. Health, fertility, grazing,
location. And farmers can now just open the app, draw a line on the app, which basically acts as a fence. So when cows
approach it, the collar beeps and vibrates, basically stopping the cows from crossing it. And with it, farmers can remotely move the whole herd to
fresh grass or the milking shed, helping them save on labor and fencing cost.
This is why 700,000 cows are already wearing these across New Zealand, US, and Australia. And Peter Theal,
co-founder of PayPal, just backed them in billion dollar valuation. The timing is also kind of bang on cuz US cattle herds are at a 75 year low and ranchers
basically cannot find workers. So a caller that saves 20 to 40 hours a week for $5 to $8 per cow per month that's going to sell itself. You know, I think
we're going to be seeing a lot more use cases of AI solving these very specific,
very niche, boring problems. And maybe that's where your big opportunity is in these boring industries nobody's looking at. I mean, who thought we'd have AI controlling cows?
